# function: nat64_extract

<!-- Edit here: this is the MarkdownDescription source for the burnham nat64_extract function. docs/functions/nat64_extract.md is generated from it by "go generate ./..."; do not edit the generated doc. -->

Recovers the IPv4 address from a NAT64 IPv6 address.

With no second argument, extracts the **last 32 bits** of the IPv6 address as a dotted-decimal IPv4 string. This is correct for the overwhelmingly common case: the Well-Known Prefix `64:ff9b::/96` and any other `/96` NAT64 prefix.

With an optional `nat64_prefix` argument (e.g. `"2001:db8::/48"`), uses the RFC 6052 byte layout for that prefix length instead, needed for `/32`–`/64` prefixes where the IPv4 bytes don't sit in the last 32 bits.

Common uses:

- Reverse-mapping NAT64 addresses in flow logs or firewall hits back to the original IPv4.
- ACL generation from IPv6 traffic records.

## Example Usage

```terraform
// Recover the IPv4 from a NAT64 IPv6 address. Default extracts the last 32 bits, correct for any /96 prefix including 64:ff9b::/96.
output "ipv4" {
  value = provider::burnham::nat64_extract("64:ff9b::192.0.2.1")
  // → "192.0.2.1"
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
nat64_extract(ipv6 string, nat64_prefix string...) string
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `ipv6` (String) The NAT64 IPv6 address to decode.
<!-- variadic argument generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `nat64_prefix` (Variadic, String) Optional: the NAT64 prefix used when the address was synthesized (e.g. "2001:db8::/48"). Required only for /32–/64 prefixes. Omit for the common /96 case.
